sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the current state and try clicking on the black shape or background
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Current white pixels:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try clicking on the black shape (node 4, which is the large black shape)
# Pick a point in the middle of the black shape, e.g., row 30, col 30
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 30}])
print(f"\nClick (30,30) on black: gameplay_changed={result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

# Try clicking on yellow background
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 10}])
print(f"Click (30,10) on yellow: gameplay_changed={result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

# Try clicking on orange background  
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 50}])
print(f"Click (30,50) on orange: gameplay_changed={result.get('gameplay_changed')}")

# Check white pixels
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  White: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
